title: Bugged Queue: Incorrect Handling of Empty Input

statement.markdown:
Implement a Queue data structure using a linked list. The `is_empty()` function should return `True` if the queue is empty and `False` otherwise. The queue should handle duplicate elements.

Example use cases:
* `q = Queue(); print(q.is_empty())  # True`
* `q.enqueue(1); print(q.is_empty())  # False`
* `q.enqueue(1); print(q.is_empty())  # False`

In this task, you will identify and fix a bug in the provided implementation. Make sure that the queue correctly handles empty input.
statement.inputFormat: (integer or list of integers) n or lst
statement.outputFormat: Boolean indicating whether the queue is empty
statement.notes: Do not return None as a valid solution. The queue should be implemented using a linked list. The queue can contain duplicate elements.

hints.text: Review the implementation of the is_empty() method and check if it returns the correct result for an empty queue

editorial.title: Correct Implementation of Queue is_empty() Method
editorial.intuition: To determine if the queue is empty, we need to check if the head of the linked list is None
editorial.bruteForce: Checking each element in the queue one by one
editorial.betterSolution: Maintaining a reference to the head of the linked list
editorial.optimalSolution: Checking if the head of the linked list is None
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The is_empty() method returns True if the head of the linked list is None and False otherwise
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the case where the queue is empty
Returning None instead of a boolean value
